title: AN ACT CREATING A BARANGAY TO BE KNOWN AS BARANGAY IPIL IN THE CITY OF TABUK, PROVINCE OF KALINGA

chunks.text:
115 OG No. 33, 8921 (August 19, 2019)

[ REPUBLIC ACT NO. 11343, April 27, 2019 ]

AN ACT CREATING A BARANGAY TO BE KNOWN AS BARANGAY IPIL IN THE CITY OF TABUK, PROVINCE OF KALINGA

Be it enacted by the Senate and House of Representatives of the Philippines in Congress assembled:

SECTION 1.

Creation of Barangay Ipil.

- The Sitio of Ipil is hereby separated from Brangay Bulanao in the City of Tabuk, Province of Kalinga to constitute a distinct and independent barangay to be known as Barangay Ipil.

SEC. 2.

.- The territorial boundaries of Barangay Ipil shall be identified as follows:

SEC. 3.

Conduct and Supervision of Plebiscite.

- The plebiscite conducted and supervised by the Commission on Elections (COMELEC) on December 21, 2002 in Barangay Bulanao pursuant to Provincial Ordinance No. 2002-02 of the Sangguniang Panlalawigan of the Province of Kalinga, which ratified the creation of Barangay Ipil, shall serve as substantial compliance with the plebiscite requirement under Section 10 of Republic Act No. 7160, as amended, otherwise known as the "Local Government Code of 1991".

SEC. 4.

Incumbent Barangay Officials.

- The incumbent barangay officials of Barangay Ipil shall continue to hold office until such time that their successors have been duly elected and qualified.

SEC. 5.

Public Infrastructure and Facilities. -

All public infrastructure and facilities for public use existing therein at the time of the approval of this Act are hereby transferred without cost or compensation to and shall be administered by the new barangay.

SEC. 6.

Internal Revenue Allotment Shares. -

Barangay Ipil shall be entitled to Internal Revenue Allotment (IRA) shares pursuant to Section 285 of Republic Act No. 7160, as amended, otherwise known as the "Local Government Code of 1991".

SEC. 7.

Effectivity. -

This Act shall take effect fifteen (15) days after its publication in the

Official Gazette

or in a newspaper of general circulation.

Approved,

(SGD.)

VICENTE C. SOTTO III

President of the Senate

(SGD.)

GLORIA MACAPAGAL ARROYO

Speaker of the House

of Representatives

This Act which originated in the House of Representatives was passed by the House of Representatives on May 15, 2017, amended by the Senate of the Philippines on February 4, 2019, and which amendments were concurred in by the House of Representatives on February 7,2019.

(SGD.)

MYRA MARIE D. VILLARICA

Secretary of the Senate

(SGD.)

DANTE ROBERTO P. MALING

Acting Secretary General

House of Representatives

Approved:

Lapsed into law on APR 27 2019 Without the signature of the President in accordance with Article VI Section 27 (1) of the Constitution

(SGD.)

RODRIGO ROA DUTERTE

President of the Philippines
